reason: Response A exhibits hallmarks of Claude Opus's distinctive prose style: dense literary allusiveness (Borges), unexpected metaphorical leaps (the "violent reduction," comparing map-reading to stepping back from a painting), and a willingness to construct paradoxes that unsettle conventional thinking. Response B, while competent and accurate, reads as a well-organized survey — the kind of informative, balanced paragraph a capable but more conservative model would produce, covering predictable ground (projections, neutrality, the map-territory distinction) in a straightforward journalistic register that lacks Opus's characteristic philosophical intensity and syntactic ambition.
